ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for lobster and crab meat soup; closest parent 'seafood soup food product' (FOODON_03301710) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for a combined lobster-crab cream soup dish; 'Crustaceans, cooked' (A0CKP) is the closest defensible parent category covering the primary protein components.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for lobster and crab meat soup as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of lobster and crab meat soup is approximately 350 g (one bowl), as typically served in Singapore seafood restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Seafood bisque / cream of seafood soup — composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central entries: Lobster, cooked (FDC ID 175180); Crab, cooked (FDC ID 175185); Heavy cream (FDC ID 170859); and standard cream soup formulation reference
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for lobster and crab meat soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a combined dish. Values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked lobster (~30% by weight), cooked crab meat (~25%), heavy cream (~15%), seafood stock (~20%), and minor ingredients (butter, flour, aromatics ~10%), consistent with a restaurant-style cream seafood soup. Energy calculated using Atwater factors. Sodium reflects typical restaurant-level seasoning. Values represent a plausible central estimate; actual restaurant preparation may vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021;114(5):1625–1632. Analogous reference: cream-based seafood soup / bisque estimated from low-carbohydrate protein-rich soup category (GI ~35–40).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lobster and crab meat soup. GI estimated at 38 based on analogous cream-based seafood soups and bisques from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. The low carbohydrate content (3.2 g/100 g) and high protein and fat content strongly attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (38 × 11.2 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 4.3, classified as low GL. gi_category set to 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~1470 mg per 350 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content (~1470 mg per bowl), which represents approximately 64% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; portion reduction or requesting low-sodium preparation is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~38) and low carbohydrate content (~3.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This soup has a low glycemic index (~38) and low carbohydrate load (~11 g per serving), making it a generally suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the cream base contributes saturated fat which should be monitored as part of overall dietary management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~68 mg/100 g) from cream and crustaceans
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The cream base and crustacean content contribute meaningful saturated fat (~9.8 g per serving) and dietary cholesterol (~238 mg per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in moderate portions, consistent with heart-healthy dietary guidelines limiting saturated fat to less than 10% of total energy intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lobster and Crab Meat Soup is a luxurious, rich cream-based seafood soup featuring generous portions of cooked lobster meat and sweet crab meat as the primary protein components, simmered in a velvety seafood stock enriched with heavy cream, butter, and aromatic vegetables such as onion and garlic. The dish is associated with Vietnamese-influenced seafood cuisine served in Singapore, as exemplified by its appearance on the menu of Viet'Spice restaurant on Circular Road. The soup has a smooth, creamy texture with a naturally sweet oceanic flavour from the crustaceans, balanced by the richness of the cream base and subtle seasoning. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, moderate-fat dish with low carbohydrate content, providing approximately 82 kcal per 100 g and around 287 kcal per standard 350 g bowl serving. The glycemic index is estimated to be low (~38), making it suitable for blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, though the saturated fat content from cream warrants attention for cardiovascular health. The sodium content is high (~420 mg/100 g), posing a concern for individuals managing hypertension. Key micronutrients include vitamin B12 from crustaceans, zinc, selenium, phosphorus, and cholesterol. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with shellfish or dairy allergies. It is typically served as a starter or main course in upscale Singapore seafood and Vietnamese-fusion restaurants, priced at the premium end of the market (~SGD 31.90 per serving).
common_user_queries: How many calories in lobster crab meat soup?
Can diabetics eat lobster crab soup?

Lobster crab soup nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact entry for lobster and crab meat soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any other primary approved regional source. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the primary ingredients (cooked lobster, cooked crab meat, heavy cream, seafood stock). GI is an analogous estimate from cream-based seafood soup category. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source database (USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimate). All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where defensible component data was available. Nutrient values represent a plausible central estimate for a restaurant-style cream lobster and crab soup; actual values will vary by restaurant preparation, cream content, and seasoning. The dish is served at Viet'Spice (Circular Road, Singapore) and is classified under Vietnamese-influenced seafood cuisine within the Singapore food context. Halal status is marked false as crustaceans (lobster, crab) are not considered halal under standard Islamic dietary law. GI category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule (GI = 38). GL per serving = (38 × 11.2) / 100 = 4.3 (low GL). Sodium per serving (~1470 mg) is a primary clinical concern for hypertensive individuals.
